subject: Chemistry
q:
Water decomposes at 2300 K

H2Og→H2g+12O2g

The percent of water decomposing at 2300 K and 1 bar is _____ (Nearest integer). Equilibrium constant for the reaction is 2×10-3 at 2300 K
question:
Water decomposes at 2300 K

H2Og→H2g+12O2g

The percent of water decomposing at 2300 K and 1 bar is _____ (Nearest integer). Equilibrium constant for the reaction is 2×10-3 at 2300 K
solution:
Decomposition of water gives:
H2Og ⇌ H2g + 12O2g

P01-α    P0α       P0α2  partial pr. at eq.

Thus, P01+α2=1            …1

Kp=PH2PO21/2PH2O

P0αP0α21/2P01-α=2×10-3

since α is negligible w.r.t 1 thus, 1-α≈1

⇒αα2=2×10-3

⇒α3/2=23/2×10-3

⇒α=23/2×2/3×10-3×2/3

⇒α=2×10-2,      %α=2%

subject: Chemistry
q:
The equilibrium composition for the reaction 

PCl3+Cl2⇌PCl5 at 298 K is given below:

PCl3eq=0.2 mol L-1, Cl2eq=0.1 mol L-1, PCl5eq=0.40 mol L-1

If 0.2 mol of Cl2 is added at the same temperature, the equilibrium concentrations of PCl5 is _____ ×10–2 mol L–1

Given: Kc for the reaction at 298 K is 20
question:
The equilibrium composition for the reaction 

PCl3+Cl2⇌PCl5 at 298 K is given below:

PCl3eq=0.2 mol L-1, Cl2eq=0.1 mol L-1, PCl5eq=0.40 mol L-1

If 0.2 mol of Cl2 is added at the same temperature, the equilibrium concentrations of PCl5 is _____ ×10–2 mol L–1

Given: Kc for the reaction at 298 K is 20
solution:
PCl3g0.2+Cl2g0.1⇌PCl5g0.4

Kc=0.40.2×0.1=20

If 0.2 moles of Cl2 is added. When reactant is added the reaction takes place in forward direction.

20=Kc=0.4+X0.3-X0.2-X

⇒0.4+X=200.3-X0.2-X

∴ 0.4+X=200.06+X2-0.5X

0.4+X=1.2+20X2-10X

20X2-11X+0.8=0

X=11±121-6440

=11-7.5540≃0.08625

∴ PCl5=0.48625≃48.625×10-2 or 49×10-2

q:

the value of equilibrium constant is 100 at 298 K. If the initial concentration of all the three species is 1 M each, then the equilibrium concentration of C is x×10-1 M. The value of x is _______. (Nearest integer)

the value of equilibrium constant is 100 at 298 K. If the initial concentration of all the three species is 1 M each, then the equilibrium concentration of C is x×10-1 M. The value of x is _______. (Nearest integer)
solution:
K=Ceq2AeqBeq=1+2x21-x1-x

100=1+2x1-x2

1+2x1-x=10

x=34

Ceq.=1+2x

=1+234

=2.5 M

=25×10-1 M

subject: Chemistry
q: One mole each of He and $A(g)$ are taken in a $10$ L closed flask and heated to $400$ K to establish the following equilibrium. $A(g) \rightleftharpoons B(g)$. $K_c$ for this reaction at $400$ K is $4.0$. The partial pressures (in atm) of He and $B(g)$ are respectively (at equilibrium) (Assume He, $A(g)$ and $B(g)$ behave as ideal gases) (Given: $R = 0.082$ L atm K$^{-1}$ mol$^{-1}$)
question: One mole each of He and $A(g)$ are taken in a $10$ L closed flask and heated to $400$ K to establish the following equilibrium. $A(g) \rightleftharpoons B(g)$. $K_c$ for this reaction at $400$ K is $4.0$. The partial pressures (in atm) of He and $B(g)$ are respectively (at equilibrium) (Assume He, $A(g)$ and $B(g)$ behave as ideal gases) (Given: $R = 0.082$ L atm K$^{-1}$ mol$^{-1}$)
solution:
The given reaction is $A(g) \rightleftharpoons B(g)$.

Let the initial moles of $A$ be $1$ and at equilibrium, let $x$ moles of $A$ dissociate.

Moles at equilibrium:
$n_A = 1 - x$
$n_B = x$

Since $\Delta n_g = 0$, the equilibrium constant $K_c$ can be written in terms of moles:
$K_c = \dfrac{n_B}{n_A} = \dfrac{x}{1 - x} = 4.0$

$x = 4 - 4x \Rightarrow 5x = 4 \Rightarrow x = 0.8$

At equilibrium, the moles of the gases are:
$n_{He} = 1$
$n_B = 0.8$

Using the ideal gas equation $P = \dfrac{nRT}{V}$, the partial pressures are calculated as follows:

Partial pressure of He:
$P_{He} = \dfrac{1 \times 0.082 \times 400}{10} = 3.28$ atm

Partial pressure of $B(g)$:
$P_B = \dfrac{0.8 \times 0.082 \times 400}{10} = 2.624$ atm

Answer: $3.28, 2.624$

subject: Chemistry
q:
At \(-20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) and \(1 \mathrm{~atm}\) pressure, a cylinder is filled with equal number of \(\mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{I}_2\) and \(\mathrm{HI}\) molecules for the reaction
\(\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{I}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{HI}(\mathrm{g})\), the \(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}\) for the process is \(x \times 10^{-1}\).
\(\mathrm{x}=\) _____
[Given : \(\mathrm{R}=0.082 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~atm} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\)]
question:
At \(-20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) and \(1 \mathrm{~atm}\) pressure, a cylinder is filled with equal number of \(\mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{I}_2\) and \(\mathrm{HI}\) molecules for the reaction
\(\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{I}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{HI}(\mathrm{g})\), the \(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}\) for the process is \(x \times 10^{-1}\).
\(\mathrm{x}=\) _____
[Given : \(\mathrm{R}=0.082 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~atm} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\)]
solution: $\begin{array}{ll}\Delta \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{g}}=0 & \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{p}}=\frac{\left(\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{HI}}\right)^2}{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2} \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{I}_2}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{T}}}{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{T}}}\right)^{\Delta \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{g}}} \\ \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{HI}}=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2}=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{I}_2} & \text { so } \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{P}}=1 \\ 1=\mathrm{x} \times 10^{-1} & \mathrm{x}=10\end{array}$

subject: Chemistry
q: At $T(K)$, the equilibrium constant of $A_2(g) + B_2(g) \rightleftharpoons C(g)$ is $2.7 \times 10^{-5}$. What is the equilibrium constant for $\dfrac{1}{3}A_2(g) + \dfrac{1}{3}B_2(g) \rightleftharpoons \dfrac{1}{3}C(g)$ at the same temperature?
question: At $T(K)$, the equilibrium constant of $A_2(g) + B_2(g) \rightleftharpoons C(g)$ is $2.7 \times 10^{-5}$. What is the equilibrium constant for $\dfrac{1}{3}A_2(g) + \dfrac{1}{3}B_2(g) \rightleftharpoons \dfrac{1}{3}C(g)$ at the same temperature?
solution:
For the reaction $A_2(g) + B_2(g) \rightleftharpoons C(g)$, the equilibrium constant is $K_1 = 2.7 \times 10^{-5}$.

When a reaction is multiplied by a factor $n$, the new equilibrium constant becomes $K^n$.

The given reaction is multiplied by $\dfrac{1}{3}$ to obtain the reaction $\dfrac{1}{3}A_2(g) + \dfrac{1}{3}B_2(g) \rightleftharpoons \dfrac{1}{3}C(g)$.

The new equilibrium constant $K_2$ is given by:

$K_2 = (K_1)^{1/3}$

$K_2 = (2.7 \times 10^{-5})^{1/3}$

$K_2 = (27 \times 10^{-6})^{1/3}$

$K_2 = 3 \times 10^{-2}$

Answer: $3 \times 10^{-2}$

q: The standard Gibbs energy change at 300 K for the reaction <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>⇌</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is 2494.2 J. At a given time, the composition of the reaction mixture is <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> and <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> . The reaction proceeds in the: <math><mo>[</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>8.314</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></mrow><mo>/</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2.718</mn><mo>] </mo></mrow></mrow></math> {Given antilog (-0.44)=0.36}
question: The standard Gibbs energy change at 300 K for the reaction <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>⇌</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is 2494.2 J. At a given time, the composition of the reaction mixture is <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> and <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> . The reaction proceeds in the: <math><mo>[</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>8.314</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></mrow><mo>/</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2.718</mn><mo>] </mo></mrow></mrow></math> {Given antilog (-0.44)=0.36}
solution:
Δ G ∘ = - RT ln K C

2494.2 = - 8.314 × 300 ln K C

2494.2 = - 8.314 × 300 × 2.303 log K C

- 2494.2 2.303 × 300 × 8.314 = - 0.44 = log K C

log KC=-0.44

K C = 0.36

Q=[B][C][A]2=2×12122=4

Q>KC, reaction will shift in reverse direction.

subject: Chemistry
q:
For the following reaction at $50°$C and at $2$ atm pressure,
$2N_2O_5(g) \rightleftharpoons 2N_2O_4(g)+O_2(g)$
$N_2O_5$ is $50\%$ dissociated.
The magnitude of standard free energy change at this temperature is $x$.
$x=$ ______ J mol$^{-1}$ [Nearest integer].
Given: $R=8.314$ J mol$^{-1}$ K$^{-1}$, $\log 2=0.30$, $\log 3=0.48$, $\ln 10=2.303$, $°C+273=K$
question:
For the following reaction at $50°$C and at $2$ atm pressure,
$2N_2O_5(g) \rightleftharpoons 2N_2O_4(g)+O_2(g)$
$N_2O_5$ is $50\%$ dissociated.
The magnitude of standard free energy change at this temperature is $x$.
$x=$ ______ J mol$^{-1}$ [Nearest integer].
Given: $R=8.314$ J mol$^{-1}$ K$^{-1}$, $\log 2=0.30$, $\log 3=0.48$, $\ln 10=2.303$, $°C+273=K$
solution:
Let the initial moles of $N_2O_5$ be $2$.

The given reaction is:
$2N_2O_5(g) \rightleftharpoons 2N_2O_4(g) + O_2(g)$

Since $N_2O_5$ is $50\%$ dissociated (degree of dissociation $\alpha = 0.5$), the moles at equilibrium are:
Moles of $N_2O_5 = 2(1 - 0.5) = 1$
Moles of $N_2O_4 = 2(0.5) = 1$
Moles of $O_2 = 0.5$

Total moles at equilibrium $= 1 + 1 + 0.5 = 2.5$

Given the total pressure $P = 2$ atm, the partial pressures of the gases are:
$P_{N_2O_5} = \dfrac{1}{2.5} \times 2 = 0.8$ atm
$P_{N_2O_4} = \dfrac{1}{2.5} \times 2 = 0.8$ atm
$P_{O_2} = \dfrac{0.5}{2.5} \times 2 = 0.4$ atm

The equilibrium constant $K_p$ is:
$K_p = \dfrac{(P_{N_2O_4})^2 \cdot P_{O_2}}{(P_{N_2O_5})^2} = \dfrac{(0.8)^2 \times 0.4}{(0.8)^2} = 0.4$

The standard free energy change $\Delta G^\circ$ is given by:
$\Delta G^\circ = -RT \ln K_p$

Using the given values:
$\ln(0.4) = 2.303 \log(0.4) = 2.303 (\log 4 - \log 10)$
$\ln(0.4) = 2.303 (2 \log 2 - 1) = 2.303 (2 \times 0.30 - 1) = 2.303 (-0.4) = -0.9212$

Now, substituting the values into the $\Delta G^\circ$ equation ($T = 50^\circ C + 273 = 323$ K):
$\Delta G^\circ = -8.314 \times 323 \times (-0.9212)$
$\Delta G^\circ = 2473.81$ J mol$^{-1}$

The magnitude of the standard free energy change to the nearest integer is $2474$ J mol$^{-1}$.

Answer: $2474$

subject: Chemistry
q:
The effect of addition of helium gas to the following reaction in equilibrium state at constant volume, is :
PCI5( g)⇌PCl3( g)+Cl2( g)
question:
The effect of addition of helium gas to the following reaction in equilibrium state at constant volume, is :
PCI5( g)⇌PCl3( g)+Cl2( g)
options: the equilibrium will shift in the forward direction and more of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Cl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> gases will be produced.
the equilibrium will go backward due to suppression of dissociation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PCl</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math>.
addition of helium will not affect the equilibrium.
solution:
PCI5( g)⇌PCl3( g)+Cl2( g)
In the above reaction vessel, when helium(an inert gas) is added following cases can happen for isobaric and isochoric processes respectively:

Case 1 : At constant P - volume will increase so reaction will shift in forward direction then answer will be A

Case 2 : At constant volume no change in active mass so reaction will not shift in any direction then answer will be D.

Thus, at constant volume, addition of helium will not have any impact on the equilibrium.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The stepwise formation of CuNH342+ is given below:

Cu2++NH3⇌K1CuNH32+

CuNH32++NH3⇌K2CuNH322+

CuNH322++NH3⇌K3CuNH332+

CuNH332++NH3⇌K4CuNH342+

The value of stability constants K1, K2, K3 and K4 are 104, 1.58×103, 5×102 and 102 respectively. The overall equilibrium constants for dissociation of CuNH342+ is x×10-12. The value of x is _____ (Rounded off to the nearest integer)
question:
The stepwise formation of CuNH342+ is given below:

Cu2++NH3⇌K1CuNH32+

CuNH32++NH3⇌K2CuNH322+

CuNH322++NH3⇌K3CuNH332+

CuNH332++NH3⇌K4CuNH342+

The value of stability constants K1, K2, K3 and K4 are 104, 1.58×103, 5×102 and 102 respectively. The overall equilibrium constants for dissociation of CuNH342+ is x×10-12. The value of x is _____ (Rounded off to the nearest integer)
solution:
Cu2++NH3⇌K1CuNH32+

CuNH32++NH3⇌K2CuNH322+

CuNH322++NH3⇌K3CuNH332+

CuNH332++NH3⇌K4CuNH342+

Cu2++4NH3⇌KCuNH342+

So,

K=K1×K2×K3×K4

=104×1.58×103×5×102×102

K=7.9×1011

Where K→ Equilibrium constant for formation of CuNH342+
So equilibrium constant K' for dissociation
 of CuNH342+ is 1 K

K'=1 K

K'=17.9×1011

=1.26×10-12=x×10-12

So the value of x=1.26

OMR Ans =1 (After rounded off to the nearest integer)

subject: Chemistry
q:
Consider the following reactions in which all the reactants and products are present in gaseous state
$2xy \rightleftharpoons x_2 + y_2 \quad K_1 = 2.5\times 10^5$
$xy + \dfrac{1}{2}z_2 \rightleftharpoons xyz \quad K_2 = 5\times 10^{-3}$
The value of $K_3$ for the equilibrium $\dfrac{1}{2}x_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}y_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}z_2 \rightleftharpoons xyz$ is:
question:
Consider the following reactions in which all the reactants and products are present in gaseous state
$2xy \rightleftharpoons x_2 + y_2 \quad K_1 = 2.5\times 10^5$
$xy + \dfrac{1}{2}z_2 \rightleftharpoons xyz \quad K_2 = 5\times 10^{-3}$
The value of $K_3$ for the equilibrium $\dfrac{1}{2}x_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}y_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}z_2 \rightleftharpoons xyz$ is:
solution:
The given reactions are:
$2xy \rightleftharpoons x_2 + y_2 \quad K_1 = 2.5 \times 10^5$
$xy + \dfrac{1}{2}z_2 \rightleftharpoons xyz \quad K_2 = 5 \times 10^{-3}$

We need to find the equilibrium constant $K_3$ for the reaction:
$\dfrac{1}{2}x_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}y_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}z_2 \rightleftharpoons xyz$

Reversing the first reaction and multiplying it by $\dfrac{1}{2}$, we get:
$\dfrac{1}{2}x_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}y_2 \rightleftharpoons xy$
The equilibrium constant for this modified reaction is:
$K' = \left(\dfrac{1}{K_1}\right)^{\dfrac{1}{2}} = \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{K_1}}$

Adding this modified reaction to the second reaction:
$\dfrac{1}{2}x_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}y_2 \rightleftharpoons xy \quad (K')$
$xy + \dfrac{1}{2}z_2 \rightleftharpoons xyz \quad (K_2)$
---------------------------------------------------
$\dfrac{1}{2}x_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}y_2 + \dfrac{1}{2}z_2 \rightleftharpoons xyz$

The equilibrium constant $K_3$ for the overall reaction is the product of the equilibrium constants of the added reactions:
$K_3 = K' \times K_2 = \dfrac{K_2}{\sqrt{K_1}}$

Substituting the given values:
$K_3 = \dfrac{5 \times 10^{-3}}{\sqrt{2.5 \times 10^5}}$

$K_3 = \dfrac{5 \times 10^{-3}}{\sqrt{25 \times 10^4}}$

$K_3 = \dfrac{5 \times 10^{-3}}{5 \times 10^2}$

$K_3 = 1.0 \times 10^{-5}$

Answer: $1.0\times 10^{-5}$

subject: Chemistry
q: In reaction $\mathrm{A}+2 \mathrm{~B} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{D}$, initial concentration of $\mathrm{B}$ was $1.5$ times of $\mathrm{[A]}$, but at equilibrium the concentrations of $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ became equal. The equilibrium constant for the reaction is :
question: In reaction $\mathrm{A}+2 \mathrm{~B} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{D}$, initial concentration of $\mathrm{B}$ was $1.5$ times of $\mathrm{[A]}$, but at equilibrium the concentrations of $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ became equal. The equilibrium constant for the reaction is :
solution: Hence $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{C}}=\frac{(2 x)^2 \times x}{(a-x)(1.5 a-2 x)^2}$ Given, at equilibrium $\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & (a-x)=(1.5 a-2 x) \\ \therefore & a=2 x\end{array}$ On solving $K_C=4$

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>⇌</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>
solution:
kp=kcRTΔng  
(i) 2HIg⇌H2g+I2g
Δng=1+1-2=0
So   kp=kc
(ii) 2NOg⇌N2g+O2g
Δng=2-2=0
So   kp=kc
(iii) NO2g+SO2g⇌NOg+SO3g
Δng=2-2=0
So   kp=kc
(iv) 2s+O2g⇌2COg
Δng=2-1=1
kp=kcRTΔn⇒kp=kcRT
kp>kc

subject: Chemistry
q:
Consider the following equilibrium
$$
\mathrm{AgCl} \downarrow+2 \mathrm{NH}_3 \rightleftharpoons\left[\mathrm{Ag}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3 2_2\right]^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right.
$$
White precipitate of $\mathrm{AgCl}$ appears on adding which of the following?
question:
Consider the following equilibrium
$$
\mathrm{AgCl} \downarrow+2 \mathrm{NH}_3 \rightleftharpoons\left[\mathrm{Ag}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3 2_2\right]^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right.
$$
White precipitate of $\mathrm{AgCl}$ appears on adding which of the following?
solution:
$2 \mathrm{HNO}_3(\mathrm{aq})+\left[\mathrm{Ag}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_2\right]^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$
$$
\longrightarrow \mathrm{AgCl}(\mathrm{s}) \downarrow+2 \mathrm{NH}_4+2 \mathrm{NO}_3^{-}
$$
When nitric acid is added to amine solution, solution is made acidic and the complex ion dissociates and liberate silver ion to recombine with chloride ion. This is the conformatory test for silver in group 1 .

subject: Chemistry
q:
$37.8 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ was taken in a 1 L reaction vessel and allowed to undergo the following reaction at 500 K
$2 \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_{5(\mathrm{~g})} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_{4(\mathrm{~g})}+\mathrm{O}_{2(\mathrm{~g})}$
The total pressure at equilibrium was found to be 18.65 bar.
Then, $\mathrm{Kp}=$ ______ $\times 10^{-2}$ [nearest integer]
Assume $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ to behave ideally under these conditions.
Given: $\mathrm{R}=0.082$ bar $\mathrm{L} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$
question:
$37.8 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ was taken in a 1 L reaction vessel and allowed to undergo the following reaction at 500 K
$2 \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_{5(\mathrm{~g})} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_{4(\mathrm{~g})}+\mathrm{O}_{2(\mathrm{~g})}$
The total pressure at equilibrium was found to be 18.65 bar.
Then, $\mathrm{Kp}=$ ______ $\times 10^{-2}$ [nearest integer]
Assume $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ to behave ideally under these conditions.
Given: $\mathrm{R}=0.082$ bar $\mathrm{L} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$
solution:
Initial pressure of $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$
$\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{\frac{37.8}{108} \times 0.082 \times 500}{1}=14.35 \text { bar } \\
& 2 \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4+\mathrm{O}_2
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{lll}\mathrm{t}=0 & 14.35 \\ \mathrm{t}=\mathrm{eq} & 14.35-2 \mathrm{P} & 2 \mathrm{P}\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{P}_{\text {Total }} \text { at eqb }=14.35+\mathrm{P}=18.65 \\ & \mathrm{P}=4.3 \\ & \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5}=5.75 \text { bar } \\ & \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4}=8.6 \mathrm{bar} \\ & \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{O}_2}=4.3 \text { bar } \\ & \mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{p}}=\frac{(8.6)^2 \times(4.3)}{(5.75)^2}=9.619=\mathrm{x} \times 10^{-2} \\ & \mathrm{x}=961.9 \approx 962\end{aligned}$

q:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction
$$
\mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})
$$
is $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=4.9 \times 10^{-2}$. The value of $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}$ for the reaction
$$
2 \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g})
$$ 
will be
question:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction
$$
\mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})
$$
is $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=4.9 \times 10^{-2}$. The value of $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}$ for the reaction
$$
2 \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g})
$$ 
will be
solution: $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}^{\prime} & =\left(\frac{1}{4.9 \times 10^{-2}}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{10^4}{4.9 \times 4.9}=\frac{100 \times 100}{24.01} \\ & =4.1649 \times 100 \\ & =416.49\end{aligned}$

q: In the sulphur estimation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>471</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of an organic compound gave <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>44</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of barium sulfate. The percentage of sulphur in the compound is. (Nearest integer) (Atomic Mass of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ba</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>137</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi></math> )
question: In the sulphur estimation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>471</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of an organic compound gave <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>44</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of barium sulfate. solution:
233 g of BaSO4 contains 32 g sulphur.

Amount of S in 1.44 g of BaSO4=32233×1.44

So percentage of S in 0.471 of organic compound =32233×1.440.471×100=42%

\(\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Y} ; \mathrm{K}_1=1.0 \\
& \mathrm{Y} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Z} ; \mathrm{K}_2=2.0 \\
& \mathrm{Z} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{W} ; \mathrm{K}_3=4.0
\end{aligned}\)
The equilibrium constant for the reaction \(\mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{W}\) is
question:
For the given hypothetical reactions, the equilibrium constants are as follows :
\(\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Y} ; \mathrm{K}_1=1.0 \\
& \mathrm{Y} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Z} ; \mathrm{K}_2=2.0 \\
& \mathrm{Z} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{W} ; \mathrm{K}_3=4.0
\end{aligned}\)
The equilibrium constant for the reaction \(\mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{W}\) is
solution:
$\begin{array}{ll}
\mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Y} & \mathrm{k}_1=1 \\
\mathrm{Y} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Z} & \mathrm{k}_2=2 \\
\mathrm{Z} \rightleftharpoons \omega & \mathrm{k}_3=4 \\
\mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \omega & \mathrm{k}_1 \cdot \mathrm{k}_2 \cdot \mathrm{k}_3 \\
& \mathrm{k}=1 \times 2 \times 4 \\
& \mathrm{k}=8
\end{array}$

subject: Chemistry
q: An open beaker of water in equilibrium with water vapour is in a sealed container. When a few grams of glucose are added to the beaker of water, the rate at which water molecules :
question: An open beaker of water in equilibrium with water vapour is in a sealed container. When a few grams of glucose are added to the beaker of water, the rate at which water molecules :
solution: The vapour pressure of solution will be less than vapour pressure of pure solvent, so some vapour molecules will get condensed to maintain new equilibrium.
